_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Communicating with people outside the organization, representing the organization to customers, the public, government, and other external sources.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in engaging with regulatory bodies during examinations, audits, and inquiries, including preparation of required documentation, coordination of response teams, and management of information requests.
comments: External communication maps to WIDAI regulatory engagement (RC-S-010), vendor management (OP-S-003), and board presentations (LS-S-006). WIDAI covers external communication in specific professional contexts.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score reflects a genuine but bounded intersection. The overlapping concepts are detectable but represent a subset of what each description covers independently.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8724 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (99.62%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is the expected NLI outcome for Intersects relationships — the descriptions share conceptual territory without one implying the other.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.3710) is close to the STS cross-encoder (0.3731). The small gap (0.00) suggests the semantic relationship is largely capturable through independent encoding — the descriptions share enough surface-level semantic features that cross-attention adds only marginal signal.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (5.71% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
